'''Nahk''' was one of two children of [[Sector Constable]] [[Noana Sowrs]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Nahk and sibling [[Tallie]] were kidnapped by [[Brejik]] and [[Griff Vao]]. The two kidnappers didn't get the chance to ask for a ransom as riots broke out on Taris stemming from the escape of [[Zayne Carrick]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] leaving the planet.

The two siblings were found when Griff's little sister, [[Mission Vao|Mission]], and Zayne, who had just returned to Taris, were spying on Griff bringing food to a storage bin in the Lower City. [[Gadon Thek]], leader of the [[Hidden Beks]], returned the children to their mother to get aid from and to help the [[Taris Resistance|resistance]] against the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] who had [[Taris Siege|invaded and taken over the planet]].

When the Mandalorians began attacking the Resistance, Noana sent the children to their father on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Noana would later fall victim to the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|rakghoul disease]].

==Behind the scenes==
When writing the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Knights of Suffering|''Knights of Suffering'']] story arc, [[John Jackson Miller]] had considered taking his own children's names and working them into those of Nahk and Tallie. However, when he started writing [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Vector|the next story arc]], he realized that their mother, Noana Sowrs, would be turning into a [[rakghoul]] and decided against basing the children on his own, giving the characters different names.<ref name="Faraway-KOTOR Countdown #10: The Naming of Knights">{{Faraway|url=blog/2010/02/12/kotor-countdown-10-naming-of-knights|text=KOTOR Countdown #10: The Naming of Knights}}</ref>
